blocks.text: ctx.sse() streams Server-Sent Events. Pass an async iterable (or a function returning one) of SSEEvent frames; it sets Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, and Connection: keep-alive automatically, and merges ctx.headers.
Each yielded value is an SSEEvent. Only data is required; non-string data is JSON-stringified before being written.

rows: Event name (the client's addEventListener type). Defaults to "message".
Event id used by the client to resume after reconnect.

blocks.text: yield { data: { hello: "world" }, event: "update", id: 1, retry: 3000 };
Use the browser's EventSource. Listen for the default message event, or for the named event you set in the frame.
SSE is one-way (server → client) and reconnects automatically. For bidirectional messaging, use a WebSocket instead — see the WebSockets page. The SSEEvent type is exported from @hushkey/howl.
